params.text:
The six safeguarding principles

The Care Act statutory guidance describes six principles that should shape every safeguarding decision and action. They help make sure our response is centred on the person, not just on the process.

  • Empowerment - supporting people to make their own decisions and giving them the information and choice to do so.
  • Prevention - acting early, before harm occurs, is always better than reacting afterwards.
  • Proportionality - the response should be the least intrusive one that is appropriate to the risk.
  • Protection - support and representation for those in greatest need.
  • Partnership - working alongside communities and other services to prevent, detect and report abuse.
  • Accountability - being transparent and taking responsibility for safeguarding practice.

Making Safeguarding Personal

Making Safeguarding Personal (MSP) puts these principles into practice. Rather than doing things to a person or deciding on their behalf, MSP means asking the person what outcome they want and shaping the response around their wishes, feelings and control over their own life. A safe outcome that ignores what the person actually wants is rarely the right one. Always ask, listen, and involve the person wherever you can.

params.taskDescription: Drag each principle to complete the sentences describing the six safeguarding principles.
params.textField:
*Empowerment* means supporting people to make their own decisions.
*Prevention* means it is better to take action before harm occurs.
*Proportionality* means giving the least intrusive response appropriate to the risk.
*Protection* means support and representation for those in greatest need.
*Partnership* means services and communities working together.
*Accountability* means being transparent and responsible for our practice.
